2604.05774.title: GenomeQA: Benchmarking General Large Language Models for Genome Sequence Understanding
2604.05774.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly adopted as conversational assistants in genomics, where they are mainly used to reason over biological knowledge, annotations, and analysis outputs through natural language interfaces. However, existing benchmarks either focus on specialized DNA models trained for sequence prediction or evaluate biological knowledge using text-only questions, leaving the behavior of general-purpose LLMs when directly exposed to raw genome sequences underexplored. We introduce GenomeQA, a benchmark designed to provide a controlled evaluation setting for general-purpose LLMs on sequence-based genome inference tasks. GenomeQA comprises 5,200 samples drawn from multiple biological databases, with sequence lengths ranging from 6 to 1,000 base pairs (bp), spanning six task families: Enhancer and Promoter Identification, Splice Site Identification, Taxonomic Classification, Histone Mark Prediction, Transcription Factor Binding Site Prediction, and TF Motif Prediction. Across six frontier LLMs, we find that models consistently outperform random baselines and can exploit local sequence signals such as GC content and short motifs, while performance degrades on tasks that require more indirect or multi-step inference over sequence patterns. GenomeQA establishes a diagnostic benchmark for studying and improving the use of general-purpose LLMs on raw genomic sequences.
2604.05774.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地被用作基因組學中的對話助手，主要用於通過自然語言介面推理生物知識、註釋和分析輸出。<lb/>然而，現有的基準要麼專注於為序列預測訓練的專用DNA模型，要麼使用僅文本問題來評估生物知識，這使得在直接接觸原始基因組序列時，通用LLMs的行為尚未得到充分探討。<lb/>我們介紹了GenomeQA，這是一個旨在為通用LLMs在基於序列的基因組推斷任務上提供受控評估環境的基準。<lb/>GenomeQA包含從多個生物數據庫中提取的5,200個樣本，序列長度範圍從6到1,000個鹼基對（bp），涵蓋六個任務類別：增強子和啟動子識別、剪接位點識別、分類學分類、組蛋白標記預測、轉錄因子結合位點預測和TF基序預測。<lb/>在六個前沿LLMs中，我們發現這些模型始終超越隨機基準，並能夠利用局部序列信號，如GC含量和短基序，而在需要更間接或多步推理的序列模式任務上，性能則會下降。<lb/>GenomeQA建立了一個診斷基準，用於研究和改善通用LLMs在原始基因組序列上的使用。
2604.05774.author: Weicai Long et.al.
2604.05774.authors: Weicai Long, Yusen Hou, Junning Feng, Houcheng Su, Shuo Yang, Donglin Xie, Yanlin Zhang
